_purpose: Forensic recompute of cross-model B_RLHF claims in paper.md §6.6 Table 3 and Figure 10, motivated by a second-pass thesis-defense audit (commit 0dc9427) that flagged a numeric divergence between paper.md and the only on-disk artifact.
_generated_by: Manual reconciliation of analysis/cross_model_results.json, docs/figure_status.md, and an exhaustive ls/grep of experiments/ for cross-model run dirs.

on_disk_artifact.contains_only: mistral-7b (2 runs each per condition; no Qwen, no GPT-4o-mini)

experiment_dir_search.result: No cross-model experiment directories found in experiments/. The only cross-model artifact on disk is analysis/cross_model_results.json (Mistral-only, no Qwen / GPT-4o-mini runs).

internal_consistency_check_under_equal_s._method: Under the equal work/save split assumption, B_RLHF = TV(π, π_uniform) = ½·(|w−⅓| + |s−⅓| + |c−⅓|) with w = s = (1−c)/2.
internal_consistency_check_under_equal_s.conclusion: Mistral Table 3 numbers are internally consistent under equal w/s split (this is how the values were 'verified' in evidence_audit.md A.4 and figure_status.md). Qwen and GPT-4o-mini Table 3 numbers are NOT internally consistent under equal split; they cannot be reproduced even from the cooperation rate alone without invoking a non-equal work/save split — which would require the full action triplet from the (non-existent) event logs.

audit_resolution.A.4_in_evidence_audit: REOPENED. The Table 3 numbers cannot be sourced from analysis/cross_model_results.json (only Mistral, magnitudes differ by 7-13x). Qwen and GPT-4o-mini rows have no on-disk data of any kind. Mistral Table 3 magnitude (-17.6%) disagrees with the only on-disk Mistral data (-84.7% from 2-run mean).
recommended_paper_actions: Reframe Table 3 / Figure 10 to clearly distinguish (a) Mistral A vs B at N=20 from analysis/cross_model_results.json (the only on-disk data) from (b) Qwen and GPT-4o-mini values which have no on-disk source.
Withdraw the headline cross-model magnitudes (-17.6 / -30.0 / +40.3) from the abstract paragraph 3 and Key Claim 2 until the runs are re-executed.
In §6.6 narrative, replace -17.6% Mistral with the JSON-derived -84.7% (or state the discrepancy directly with both values).

In §3.2.1 Conjecture, retain only the directional claim 'B_RLHF(A) > 0 across families' as supported by the existing on-disk Mistral data; remove the magnitude-dependent claims that rely on Qwen/GPT-4o-mini numbers.

Add Limitation 18 (or extend Limitation 14) noting that the cross-model results (paper.md §6.6, Figure 10) have unverifiable provenance for Qwen and GPT-4o-mini, and that the only auditable Mistral N=20 result shows a much larger grounding effect (~85% reduction) than the paper currently claims (-17.6%), but both interpretations are consistent with the §8.1 N=100 null only if the effect's magnitude is highly N-sensitive.
recommended_engineering_actions: Re-execute scripts/run_cross_model_comparison.py for all three model families with the patched code (post commit c73554e) to regenerate analysis/cross_model_results.json with the full A/B grid.

Verify that any pre-2026-05-23 cross-model figures cited in paper.md are reproducible from the patched-code re-run; if not, treat them like the pilot phase_c_comparison figures (descriptive of pre-patch single runs only).
